heading: Gifts
label: 4352 Gifts
text: The University and NTID are authorized to receive by gift, devise, bequest, purchase, or otherwise, property, both real and personal, for the use of the University or NTID, or for the use, as appropriate, for any programs, departments, or other units as may be designated in the conveyance or will, and to hold, invest, use, or dispose of such property for the purpose stated in the conveyance or will.
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior was renumbered section 112 and is classified to .section 202 of Pub. L. 99–371section 4332 of this titleProvisions similar to subsec. (a) of this section were contained in prior to repeal by .section 691c of this titlePub. L. 99–371

Amendments1992—, amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows:Pub. L. 102–421, § 132“(a) .—Gallaudet University is authorized to receive by gift, devise, bequest, purchase, or otherwise, property, both real and personal, for the use of Gallaudet University, or for the use of any of its departments or other units as may be designated in the conveyance or will, and to hold, invest, use, or dispose of such property for the purpose stated in the conveyance or will.Gallaudet University“(b) .—The National Technical Institute for the Deaf is authorized to receive by gift, devise, bequest, purchase, or otherwise, property, both real and personal, for the use of the Institute, or for the use of any of its programs as may be designated in the conveyance or will, and to hold, invest, use, or dispose of such property for the purpose stated in the conveyance or will.”National Technical Institute for the Deaf
Effective Date of 1992 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 102–421section 161 of Pub. L. 102–421section 4301 of this titleOct. 1, 1992
